profiles.label: Fanchen Meng CV: computational materials science, AI/HPC, and research systems
profiles.summary: CV-derived profile: Ph.D. in condensed matter physics and materials science background with experience at Clemson and Brookhaven. Research and software strengths include computational materials science, first-principles electronic structure, thermoelectric transport, electron-phonon and phonon-phonon interactions, Boltzmann transport, lattice thermal conductivity, X-ray absorption spectroscopy workflows, VASP, Quantum Espresso, Fortran/Python/MPI, HPC research computing, AI/ML for scientific applications, analytics systems, and research software such as Lightshow, ShengBTE, BTE4EPI, and EPI4LTC.

profiles.label: Zhenzhen Zhang CV: computational biophysics, amyloid, protein/RNA modeling
profiles.summary: CV-derived profile: Ph.D. biophysics researcher using discrete molecular dynamics simulations and molecular modeling to study functional biomolecules. Research areas include amyloid aggregation, protein folding and fibrillization, liquid-liquid phase separation, amyloid peptides ABri/ADan/IAPP/A beta/FapC, BRICHOS inhibition, protein-protein interactions, protein corona, RNA structure prediction through RNA-Puzzles, PyMOL, Bash/Python data analysis, biomolecular phase transitions, and computational biophysics education.

profiles.label: Quantum computing and quantum information systems
profiles.cv_source: User-requested extension aligned with quantum/materials/HPC profile
profiles.summary: Funding-fit extension for quantum computing, quantum information science, quantum algorithms, quantum simulation, quantum materials, quantum sensing, and HPC/AI-enabled quantum systems. Treat quantum opportunities as potentially relevant when they connect to computational physics, materials, simulation, algorithms, scientific computing, or cyberinfrastructure.
